title: Lawmakers want to ban AI companies from selling your health data
slides: What if your therapy data gets sold without consent?
New bill targets AI firms trading health records.
Even anonymized data can be reidentified easily.
You might lose control over your own medical privacy.
Check your provider’s policy before sharing anything.
thread_text:
1. Your data sold without consent

A new bill targets AI firms that trade health records. Even anonymized data can be reidentified easily.

2. Reidentification is simple

Researchers have proven that anonymized health data can be matched with public records in minutes.

3. You might lose control

Once sold, your therapy notes could be used to deny insurance or employment.

4. No opt-out exists yet

Current laws don't require your consent for AI training on medical data.

5. Check your provider’s policy

Ask your therapist or clinic: do they share data with third parties? If you haven’t checked, do it this week.
video_script: Your therapy data could be sold without consent.

script: Your therapy data could be sold without consent.
A new bill targets AI firms trading health records.
Even anonymized data can be reidentified easily.
You might lose control over your medical privacy.
Check your provider’s policy before sharing anything.

instagram.caption:
Your therapy notes could be sold to insurers without you ever knowing. 🏥🔍

A new bill wants to stop AI companies from trading your health records. Even data labeled "anonymous" can be matched back to you in minutes using public records. Once sold, those records could be used to deny you a job or a policy. ❗

If you use AI tools for work or content, your medical privacy isn't safe unless you check your provider's fine print. 

Have you read your clinic's data-sharing policy this week?

carousel_slides.hook: Your therapy data sold without consent?
carousel_slides.description: A new bill targets AI firms that trade health records. Anonymized data can be reidentified easily.
Researchers matched anonymized health data with public records in minutes, exposing your identity.

carousel_slides.hook: Could your therapy notes deny you insurance?
carousel_slides.description: Once sold, your health data might be used to block employment or increase premiums.

carousel_slides.hook: No opt-out exists for AI training.
carousel_slides.description: Current laws don't require your consent for AI to train on private medical records.
